---
title: "Bizard Skills"
subtitle: "AI-Ready Visualization Skills for Biomedical Research"
from: markdown+emoji
---

## What are Bizard Skills? :brain:

**Bizard Skills** convert every tutorial in this atlas into a compact, structured **AI skill document** — a plain-text summary of what a chart is, when to use it, what R packages it needs, and the minimal reproducible code to produce it. Skills can be:

- Copied directly into AI assistant context windows (ChatGPT, Copilot, Claude, etc.)
- Used by local LLM tools like [LM Studio](https://lmstudio.ai/) or [Ollama](https://ollama.ai/)
- Downloaded as a ZIP for offline use in custom AI pipelines
- Referenced by automated bioinformatics workflows

::: callout-tip
**How to use a skill**: Click any card below to view the skill document, then copy its content into your AI assistant prompt. For example: *"Use the following skill to help me create a violin plot comparing gene expression between tumor and normal samples."*
:::

---

## Browse Skills by Category {#browse}

Use the search box or click a category to filter skills.

```{=html}
<div id="skills-browser" style="margin-bottom:2rem;">

  <!-- Search + filter bar -->
  <div style="display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin-bottom:1rem;">
    <input id="skill-search" type="text" placeholder="Search skills…" oninput="filterSkills()"
      style="flex:1;min-width:200px;padding:.5rem .8rem;font-size:.95rem;border:1px solid #ccc;border-radius:6px;font-family:inherit;">
    <div id="cat-filters" style="display:flex;gap:.4rem;flex-wrap:wrap;"></div>
  </div>

  <!-- Skills grid -->
  <div id="skills-grid" style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fill,minmax(240px,1fr));gap:.8rem;"></div>

  <!-- Skill detail modal -->
  <div id="skill-modal" style="display:none;position:fixed;inset:0;background:rgba(0,0,0,.45);z-index:9999;overflow-y:auto;padding:2rem 1rem;">
    <div style="max-width:720px;margin:auto;background:#fff;border-radius:10px;padding:1.8rem 2rem;position:relative;">
      <button onclick="document.getElementById('skill-modal').style.display='none'"
        style="position:absolute;top:1rem;right:1rem;background:#f0f0f0;border:none;border-radius:4px;
               padding:.3rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font-size:1rem;">✕</button>
      <h3 id="modal-title" style="margin-top:0;"></h3>
      <div style="margin-bottom:.6rem;">
        <span id="modal-category" style="font-size:.8rem;padding:.2rem .6rem;border-radius:12px;color:#fff;font-weight:600;"></span>
      </div>
      <pre id="modal-content" style="background:#f7f7f7;padding:1rem;border-radius:6px;
           font-size:.82rem;overflow-x:auto;white-space:pre-wrap;max-height:55vh;overflow-y:auto;"></pre>
      <div style="margin-top:1rem;display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;">
        <button onclick="copySkill()" style="background:#3a86ff;color:#fff;border:none;padding:.5rem 1.1rem;
          border-radius:6px;cursor:pointer;font-weight:600;">📋 Copy Skill</button>
        <a id="modal-tutorial-link" href="#" target="_blank" style="
          background:#06d6a0;color:#fff;border:none;padding:.5rem 1.1rem;
          border-radius:6px;cursor:pointer;font-weight:600;text-decoration:none;
          display:inline-flex;align-items:center;">📖 Open Tutorial</a>
        <button id="download-btn" onclick="downloadSkill()" style="
          background:#f0f0f0;color:#333;border:1px solid #ccc;padding:.5rem 1rem;
          border-radius:6px;cursor:pointer;">⬇ Download</button>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<script>
// ── Bizard skills database ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Each entry contains a compact skill document template.
const SKILLS = [
  {
    name: "Violin Plot",
    category: "Distribution",
    url: "Distribution/ViolinPlot.html",
    packages: ["ggplot2", "ggpubr"],
    useWhen: "Comparing the distribution of a continuous variable (e.g. gene expression) across two or more groups. Shows data density + spread + outliers simultaneously.",
    skill: `# Skill: Violin Plot (R/ggplot2)

## When to use
Compare continuous variable distributions across groups (e.g. gene expression between
tumor/normal, treatment/control). Best for n > 20 per group.

## Key packages
- ggplot2 (core plotting)
- ggpubr (statistical annotations)

## Minimal reproducible example
\`\`\`r
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)

ggplot(iris, aes(x = Species, y = Sepal.Length, fill = Species)) +
  geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, fill = "white", outlier.size = 0.5) +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = list(c("setosa","versicolor"),
                                        c("versicolor","virginica")),
                     method = "wilcox.test") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(title = "Violin Plot", x = "Species", y = "Sepal Length (cm)")
\`\`\`

## Customization tips
- Add jitter points: + geom_jitter(width=0.1, alpha=0.3)
- Remove legend: + theme(legend.position="none")
- Horizontal: + coord_flip()

## Full tutorial: https://openbiox.github.io/Bizard/Distribution/ViolinPlot.html`
  },
  {
    name: "Box Plot",
    category: "Distribution",
    url: "Distribution/BoxPlot.html",
    packages: ["ggplot2", "ggpubr"],
    useWhen: "Comparing medians, quartiles, and outliers of a continuous variable across groups. Standard choice for clinical data, biomarker comparison.",
    skill: `# Skill: Box Plot (R/ggplot2)

## When to use
Compare medians/quartiles/outliers of a continuous variable across groups.
Standard for clinical biomarker data; works with small and large datasets.

## Key packages
- ggplot2, ggpubr

## Minimal reproducible example
\`\`\`r
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)

ggplot(iris, aes(x = Species, y = Sepal.Length, fill = Species)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = 19, outlier.size = 1.5) +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = list(c("setosa","versicolor"),
                                        c("versicolor","virginica")),
                     method = "wilcox.test", label = "p.signif") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Sepal Length (cm)")
\`\`\`

## Full tutorial: https://openbiox.github.io/Bizard/Distribution/BoxPlot.html`
  },
  {
    name: "Volcano Plot",
    category: "Omics",
    url: "Omics/VolcanoPlot.html",
    packages: ["ggplot2", "ggrepel", "EnhancedVolcano"],
    useWhen: "Visualizing differential expression results (RNA-seq, proteomics, metabolomics). Shows fold-change vs significance for thousands of features simultaneously.",
    skill: `# Skill: Volcano Plot (R/ggplot2)

## When to use
Display differential expression or differential abundance results (RNA-seq, proteomics,
metabolomics). Plots -log10(p-value) vs log2(fold-change) to simultaneously show
statistical significance and biological effect size for all features.

## Key packages
- ggplot2, ggrepel (labels), EnhancedVolcano (advanced)

## Minimal reproducible example
\`\`\`r
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

# df has columns: gene, log2FC, padj
set.seed(42)
df <- data.frame(
  gene   = paste0("Gene", 1:500),
  log2FC = rnorm(500, 0, 2),
  padj   = runif(500, 1e-10, 1)
)
df$sig <- with(df, ifelse(padj < 0.05 & abs(log2FC) > 1,
                          ifelse(log2FC > 1, "Up", "Down"), "NS"))

ggplot(df, aes(log2FC, -log10(padj), color = sig)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(Up="#e63946", Down="#457b9d", NS="grey70")) +
  geom_text_repel(data = subset(df, sig != "NS")[1:15,],
                  aes(label = gene), size = 2.5, max.overlaps = 20) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Volcano Plot", x = "log2(Fold Change)", y = "-log10(padj)")
\`\`\`

## Full tutorial: https://openbiox.github.io/Bizard/Omics/VolcanoPlot.html`
  },
  {
    name: "Heatmap",
    category: "Correlation",
    url: "Correlation/Heatmap.html",
    packages: ["pheatmap", "ComplexHeatmap"],
    useWhen: "Visualizing a matrix of values (gene expression, correlation, methylation). Reveals patterns, clusters, and gradients across rows/columns.",
    skill: `# Skill: Heatmap (R/pheatmap)

## When to use
Visualize a matrix of numeric values: gene expression profiles, correlation matrices,
methylation data, drug response matrices. Use clustering (rows/cols) to reveal patterns.

## Key packages
- pheatmap (simple), ComplexHeatmap (advanced annotations)

## Minimal reproducible example
\`\`\`r
library(pheatmap)

# Use the built-in mtcars dataset
mat <- scale(t(mtcars[1:20, 1:8]))  # scale each gene

pheatmap(
  mat,
  scale          = "none",
  cluster_rows   = TRUE,
  cluster_cols   = TRUE,
  color          = colorRampPalette(c("#2166ac","white","#d73027"))(100),
  fontsize       = 9,
  main           = "Sample Heatmap"
)
\`\`\`

## Customization tips
- Add column annotations: use annotation_col argument
- Custom color breaks: use breaks argument
- For large matrices (>5000 genes): use ComplexHeatmap for performance

## Full tutorial: https://openbiox.github.io/Bizard/Correlation/Heatmap.html`
  },
  {
    name: "TextEnrichment BarPlot",
    category: "Omics",
    url: "Omics/TextEnrichmentBarPlot.html",
    packages: ["ggplot2", "ggprism", "gground"],
    useWhen: "Displaying GO/KEGG enrichment results with text overlaid on rounded bars. Ideal for single-cell marker gene enrichment or any functional enrichment with many pathways.",
    skill: `# Skill: Text-Overlaid Enrichment BarPlot (R/ggplot2)

## When to use
Display functional enrichment results (GO, KEGG) with pathway names directly overlaid
on rounded bars. Best for single-cell subpopulation marker enrichment or any analysis
requiring simultaneous display of pathway significance + driver genes.

## Key packages
- ggplot2, ggprism (theme), gground (rounded bars via geom_round_col/geom_round_rect)

## Install gground
\`\`\`r
remotes::install_github("dxsbiocc/gground")
\`\`\`

## Minimal reproducible example
\`\`\`r
library(ggplot2); library(ggprism); library(gground)

# Example enrichment data (replace with your clusterProfiler output)
df <- data.frame(
  Description = paste("Pathway", 1:10),
  p.adjust    = c(0.001,0.003,0.005,0.008,0.012,0.015,0.02,0.03,0.04,0.049),
  Category    = rep(c("BP","KEGG"), 5)
) |> dplyr::mutate(
  Category    = factor(Category),
  Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(Description)),
  index       = seq_len(nrow(df))
)

ggplot(df, aes(-log10(p.adjust), y = index, fill = Category)) +
  geom_round_col(aes(y = Description), width = 0.6, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(x = 0.05, label = Description), hjust = 0, size = 3.5) +
  labs(x = "-log10(p.adjust)", y = NULL) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(BP="#4895ef", KEGG="#f72585")) +
  theme_prism() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.line.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank())
\`\`\`

## Full tutorial: https://openbiox.github.io/Bizard/Omics/TextEnrichmentBarPlot.html`
  },
  {
    name: "Cell-Cell Communication Circle Plot",
    category: "Omics",
    url: "Omics/CellChatCirclePlot.html",
    packages: ["CellChat"],
    useWhen: "Visualizing ligand-receptor mediated cell-cell communication inferred from scRNA-seq data. Shows sender-receiver relationships and interaction strengths between cell types.",
    skill: `# Skill: Cell-Cell Communication Circle Plot (R/CellChat)

## When to use
Visualize ligand-receptor mediated cell-cell communication networks from single-cell
RNA-seq data. Shows which cell types send/receive signals and how strongly they interact.

## Key packages
- CellChat (install from GitHub: jinworks/CellChat)

## Minimal reproducible example (requires CellChat object)
\`\`\`r
library(CellChat)

# Assuming 'cellchat' is a pre-built CellChat object with aggregateNet() run
groupSize <- as.numeric(table(cellchat@idents))

par(mfrow = c(1,2), xpd = TRUE)

# Number of interactions
netVisual_circle(cellchat@net$count, vertex.weight = groupSize,
                 weight.scale = TRUE, title.name = "Number of interactions")

# Interaction strength
netVisual_circle(cellchat@net$weight, vertex.weight = groupSize,
                 weight.scale = TRUE, title.name = "Interaction weights/strength")
\`\`\`

## Build CellChat object (reference)
\`\`\`r
cellchat <- createCellChat(object = expr_matrix, meta = meta_df, group.by = "cell_type")
cellchat@DB <- CellChatDB.human
cellchat <- subsetData(cellchat)
cellchat <- identifyOverExpressedGenes(cellchat)
cellchat <- identifyOverExpressedInteractions(cellchat)
cellchat <- computeCommunProb(cellchat)
cellchat <- filterCommunication(cellchat, min.cells = 10)
cellchat <- computeCommunProbPathway(cellchat)
cellchat <- aggregateNet(cellchat)
\`\`\`

## Full tutorial: https://openbiox.github.io/Bizard/Omics/CellChatCirclePlot.html`
  },
  {
    name: "MEBOCOST Flow Plot",
    category: "Omics",
    url: "Omics/MebocostFlowPlot.html",
    packages: ["ggplot2", "ggraph", "igraph", "tidygraph"],
    useWhen: "Visualizing metabolite-mediated intercellular communication from scRNA-seq. Shows which cell types produce and sense metabolites and the strength of metabolic crosstalk.",
    skill: `# Skill: MEBOCOST Metabolic Flow Plot (R/ggraph)

## When to use
Visualize metabolite-mediated cell-cell communication inferred from scRNA-seq data
using MEBOCOST. Shows metabolic flux between cell types (sender = metabolite producer,
receiver = metabolite sensor). Complements protein-based communication (CellChat).

## Key packages
- ggraph, igraph, tidygraph (for the flow network visualization)
- MEBOCOST (GitHub: kaifuchenlab/MEBOCOST) for inference

## Minimal reproducible example
\`\`\`r
library(ggraph); library(igraph); library(tidygraph); library(dplyr)

# comm_df: data frame with columns sender, receiver, score
# (output from MEBOCOST aggregated communication table)

g <- graph_from_data_frame(
  d = comm_df %>% rename(weight = score), directed = TRUE,
  vertices = data.frame(name = unique(c(comm_df$sender, comm_df$receiver)))
)

set.seed(42)
ggraph(g, layout = "circle") +
  geom_edge_arc(aes(width = weight, alpha = weight),
                arrow = arrow(length = unit(3,"mm"), type = "closed"),
                end_cap = circle(6,"mm"), color = "#3a86ff", curvature = 0.2) +
  geom_node_point(size = 12, fill = "#fb5607", shape = 21, color = "white", stroke = 2) +
  geom_node_label(aes(label = name), size = 3, fontface = "bold",
                  color = "white", fill = NA, label.size = 0) +
  scale_edge_width_continuous(range = c(0.5, 4)) +
  scale_edge_alpha_continuous(range = c(0.3, 1), guide = "none") +
  theme_graph() +
  labs(title = "Metabolic Cell-Cell Communication Flow")
\`\`\`

## Full tutorial: https://openbiox.github.io/Bizard/Omics/MebocostFlowPlot.html`
  },
  {
    name: "Kaplan-Meier Survival Plot",
    category: "Clinics",
    url: "Clinics/KaplanMeierPlot.html",
    packages: ["survminer", "survival"],
    useWhen: "Displaying time-to-event data (overall survival, progression-free survival) stratified by a clinical variable or molecular subgroup. Standard for cancer clinical research.",
    skill: `# Skill: Kaplan-Meier Survival Plot (R/survminer)

## When to use
Display time-to-event (survival) data stratified by groups (e.g. high/low expression,
treatment arm, molecular subtype). Standard for OS, PFS, DFS in cancer research.

## Key packages
- survival (core), survminer (publication-quality KM plots)

## Minimal reproducible example
\`\`\`r
library(survival)
library(survminer)

# Use built-in lung dataset
lung$status2 <- ifelse(lung$status == 2, 1, 0)

fit <- survfit(Surv(time, status2) ~ sex, data = lung)

ggsurvplot(
  fit,
  data          = lung,
  pval          = TRUE,
  conf.int      = TRUE,
  risk.table    = TRUE,
  palette       = c("#e63946","#457b9d"),
  xlab          = "Time (days)",
  ylab          = "Survival probability",
  legend.labs   = c("Male","Female"),
  title         = "Overall Survival by Sex"
)
\`\`\`

## Full tutorial: https://openbiox.github.io/Bizard/Clinics/KaplanMeierPlot.html`
  },
  {
    name: "PCA Plot",
    category: "Correlation",
    url: "Correlation/PCAplot.html",
    packages: ["FactoMineR", "factoextra", "ggplot2"],
    useWhen: "Reducing high-dimensional omics data to 2D for quality control, batch effect detection, and sample clustering. First step in any omics exploratory analysis.",
    skill: `# Skill: PCA Plot (R/FactoMineR + factoextra)

## When to use
Dimensionality reduction for high-dimensional omics data (RNA-seq, proteomics, metabolomics).
Use for QC (detect outliers, batch effects), sample clustering, and exploration before
downstream differential analysis.

## Key packages
- FactoMineR (PCA), factoextra (visualization), ggplot2

## Minimal reproducible example
\`\`\`r
library(FactoMineR)
library(factoextra)

pca_res <- PCA(iris[,1:4], graph = FALSE)

fviz_pca_ind(
  pca_res,
  geom.ind   = "point",
  col.ind    = iris$Species,
  palette    = c("#e63946","#457b9d","#2a9d8f"),
  addEllipses = TRUE,
  ellipse.type = "confidence",
  legend.title = "Species"
) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "PCA of Iris Dataset")
\`\`\`

## Full tutorial: https://openbiox.github.io/Bizard/Correlation/PCAplot.html`
  },
];

// ── Render skills grid ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
const catColors = {
  Distribution:'#4895ef', Correlation:'#4cc9f0', Ranking:'#f72585',
  Composition:'#7209b7',  Proportion:'#3a0ca3',  Evolution:'#4361ee',
  Omics:'#06d6a0',        Clinics:'#ef476f'
};

function renderSkills(skills) {
  const grid = document.getElementById('skills-grid');
  grid.innerHTML = '';
  skills.forEach((s, i) => {
    const color = catColors[s.category] || '#999';
    const card = document.createElement('div');
    card.style.cssText = `border:1px solid #e0e0e0;border-left:4px solid ${color};border-radius:8px;
      padding:1rem 1.1rem;background:#fff;cursor:pointer;transition:box-shadow .15s;`;
    card.onmouseover = () => card.style.boxShadow = '0 2px 10px rgba(0,0,0,.12)';
    card.onmouseout  = () => card.style.boxShadow = 'none';
    card.onclick     = () => openSkill(i);
    card.innerHTML = `
      <div style="font-weight:700;font-size:.95rem;margin-bottom:.3rem;">${s.name}</div>
      <span style="background:${color};color:#fff;font-size:.72rem;padding:.15rem .5rem;border-radius:10px;">${s.category}</span>
      <p style="font-size:.82rem;color:#555;margin:.5rem 0 0;">${s.useWhen.slice(0,90)}…</p>
      <div style="margin-top:.6rem;font-size:.78rem;color:#888;">
        📦 ${s.packages.slice(0,3).join(', ')}${s.packages.length>3?'…':''}
      </div>`;
    grid.appendChild(card);
  });
  if (skills.length === 0) {
    grid.innerHTML = '<p style="color:#888;grid-column:1/-1;">No skills match your search.</p>';
  }
}

// ── Category filter buttons ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
const cats = [...new Set(SKILLS.map(s => s.category))];
let activeCat = null;
const filterDiv = document.getElementById('cat-filters');
cats.forEach(cat => {
  const color = catColors[cat] || '#999';
  const btn = document.createElement('button');
  btn.textContent = cat;
  btn.dataset.cat = cat;
  btn.style.cssText = `border:2px solid ${color};color:${color};background:#fff;padding:.25rem .7rem;
    border-radius:12px;font-size:.8rem;cursor:pointer;font-weight:600;transition:all .15s;`;
  btn.onclick = () => {
    activeCat = activeCat === cat ? null : cat;
    document.querySelectorAll('#cat-filters button').forEach(b => {
      const bc = catColors[b.dataset.cat]||'#999';
      b.style.background = (b.dataset.cat === activeCat) ? bc : '#fff';
      b.style.color      = (b.dataset.cat === activeCat) ? '#fff' : bc;
    });
    filterSkills();
  };
  filterDiv.appendChild(btn);
});

function filterSkills() {
  const q = document.getElementById('skill-search').value.toLowerCase();
  let filtered = SKILLS;
  if (activeCat) filtered = filtered.filter(s => s.category === activeCat);
  if (q) filtered = filtered.filter(s =>
    s.name.toLowerCase().includes(q) ||
    s.category.toLowerCase().includes(q) ||
    s.useWhen.toLowerCase().includes(q) ||
    s.packages.some(p => p.toLowerCase().includes(q))
  );
  renderSkills(filtered);
}

// ── Skill detail modal ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
let currentSkill = null;
function openSkill(idx) {
  // Use the same filter logic as filterSkills() to resolve the correct skill
  const q = document.getElementById('skill-search').value.toLowerCase();
  let filtered = SKILLS;
  if (activeCat) filtered = filtered.filter(s => s.category === activeCat);
  if (q) filtered = filtered.filter(s =>
    s.name.toLowerCase().includes(q) ||
    s.category.toLowerCase().includes(q) ||
    s.useWhen.toLowerCase().includes(q) ||
    s.packages.some(p => p.toLowerCase().includes(q))
  );
  currentSkill = filtered[idx];
  if (!currentSkill) return;

  document.getElementById('modal-title').textContent = currentSkill.name;
  const badge = document.getElementById('modal-category');
  badge.textContent = currentSkill.category;
  badge.style.background = catColors[currentSkill.category] || '#999';
  document.getElementById('modal-content').textContent = currentSkill.skill;
  document.getElementById('modal-tutorial-link').href = currentSkill.url;
  document.getElementById('skill-modal').style.display = 'block';
}

function copySkill() {
  if (!currentSkill) return;
  navigator.clipboard.writeText(currentSkill.skill)
    .then(() => { const btn = document.querySelector('#skill-modal button'); btn.textContent = '✓ Copied!'; setTimeout(()=>btn.textContent='📋 Copy Skill',2000); })
    .catch(() => { /* fallback */ const ta=document.createElement('textarea'); ta.value=currentSkill.skill; document.body.appendChild(ta); ta.select(); document.execCommand('copy'); document.body.removeChild(ta); });
}

function downloadSkill() {
  if (!currentSkill) return;
  const blob = new Blob([currentSkill.skill], {type:'text/plain'});
  const a = document.createElement('a');
  a.href = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
  a.download = currentSkill.name.replace(/[^a-z0-9]/gi,'_').toLowerCase() + '_skill.md';
  a.click();
}

// Close modal on backdrop click
document.getElementById('skill-modal').addEventListener('click', function(e) {
  if (e.target === this) this.style.display = 'none';
});

// Initial render
renderSkills(SKILLS);
</script>
```

---

## Download All Skills {#download}

::: callout-note
Use the script at `.github/scripts/generate_skills.py` to regenerate skill documents from all QMD tutorials automatically. Run:
```bash
python .github/scripts/generate_skills.py --output skills/
```
:::

The auto-generated skill files follow this naming convention: `<TutorialName>_skill.md`

Each skill document contains:
- **Chart name and category**
- **When to use** — a concise decision guide  
- **Key R packages** required
- **Minimal reproducible R code** to produce the chart
- **Link** back to the full tutorial
